chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-348 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For katcha peshi. 07.08.2017 None present for the petitioner. Mr. Anwar H. Ansari, State Counsel a/w ASI Bashir Ahmed of P.S Khuda Abad, Dadu and SHO Kotri. = With reference to the order of previous date, learned Additional A.G files statement on behalf of the respondent No.3, which is taken on record. Through this statement, it has been mentioned that the petitioner and the private respondents are close relatives to each other. It is also stated that Sikandar Ali S/o Faiz Muhammad (son of petitioner) has contracted marriage with Mst.Erum D/o Ali Murad Lund and she is residing with her husband, however, due to some domestic dispute, Mst.Erum left the house of her husband and filed the suit for dissolution of marriage before the Family Court, Jamshoro at Kotri. This act of Mst.Erum annoyed the petitioner, who filed the present petition. It is also stated in the statement that the respondent No.3 undertakes to provide protection to the citizen as well as to the petitioner as required under the law. Learned Counsel for the petitioner has no objection for the disposal of the present petition, if directions are given to the respondents not to cause harassment to the petitioner. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with the directions to the respondents not to cause harassment to the petitioner and further that the police officials should discharge their duties in accordance with law and provide protection to the petitioner, whenever he approaches them with such request, but strictly in accordance with law.

Learned Counsel for the petitioner has no objection for the disposal of the present petition, if directions are given to the respondents not to cause harassment to the petitioner. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with the directions to the respondents not to cause harassment to the petitioner and further that the police officials should discharge their duties in accordance with law and provide protection to the petitioner, whenever he approaches them with such request, but strictly in accordance with law. JUDGE Shahid
